text.name: setting_moisture_environment_tooltip
text.text: Sets the climate type for moisture calculations

text.name: setting_moisture_moistureLossMultiplier_tooltip
text.text: Controls the speed of moisture loss (1-10)

text.name: setting_moisture_moistureGainMultiplier_tooltip
text.text: Controls the speed of moisture gain from rain (1-10)

text.name: setting_moisture_teddingMoistureReduction_tooltip
text.text: Amount of moisture reduced per tedding pass (1-5%)

text.name: setting_moisture_baleRotEnabled_tooltip
text.text: Enable bale rotting during rain/snow. Wrapped bales and bales under shelter are protected.

text.name: setting_moisture_baleRotRate_tooltip
text.text: Multiplier for bale volume loss rate. Higher values mean faster rotting.

text.name: setting_moisture_baleExposureDecayRate_tooltip
text.text: How fast bales dry out after rain stops. Lower = slower drying.

text.name: setting_moisture_qualityDecayMultiplier_tooltip
text.text: How fast crop quality degrades when stored above ideal moisture. Higher = faster decay.

text.name: setting_moisture_dryingSpeed_tooltip
text.text: Moisture percentage removed per hour by silo drying.

text.name: setting_moisture_sellDryingChargeRate_tooltip
text.text: Drying charge multiplier applied when selling wet crops. Higher = more expensive.

text.name: setting_moisture_showFieldMoisture_tooltip
text.text: Display the current moisture level at your position in the field info HUD.

text.name: setting_moisture_moistureMeterReporting_tooltip
text.text: How the moisture meter displays readings.

text.name: ms_drying_complete
text.text: Drying complete - all crops at ideal moisture

text.name: moistureSystem_gui_description
text.text: The data below shows moisture grading for each supported crop. The moisture range for the grade is shown, and the price multiplier for that grade is shown in brackets.

text.name: moistureSystem_gui_moistureToQualityDesc
text.text: Shows the quality value assigned at harvest based on moisture level. Harvest within the ideal range (Grade A) for maximum quality. Quality degrades hourly when stored above ideal moisture.

text.name: moistureSystem_gui_qualityToPriceDesc
text.text: Shows the sell price multiplier based on crop quality. Quality ranges vary per crop - some are more resilient to quality loss than others.

text.name: moistureSystem_gui_calendarDescription
text.text: The calendar below shows the expected moisture range for each month based on your current environment setting. Moisture levels are influenced by rainfall, temperature, and season.

text.name: help_moisture_overview_1
text.text: The Moisture System mod introduces dynamic moisture levels and crop quality tracking that affect crops, grass, and bales throughout your farm. Moisture varies based on terrain elevation, weather conditions, and time of day. Quality is determined at harvest and degrades over time if crops are stored too wet.

text.name: help_moisture_overview_2
text.text: This creates a more realistic farming experience where harvest timing, drying, and storage management matter. Use the Moisture Menu (Shift+M) to view quality-to-price tables, moisture-to-quality charts, and moisture calendars.

text.name: help_moisture_field_1
text.text: Field moisture varies across your farm based on several factors: Terrain elevation (lower areas are wetter), weather conditions (rain and snow increase moisture), temperature (warmer weather accelerates drying), and time of day (moisture decreases more during daytime).

text.name: help_moisture_field_2
text.text: Monthly moisture ranges change throughout the year based on your chosen environment type (Dry/Normal/Wet). Use the moisture meter hand tool or enable the HUD display in settings to monitor moisture levels.

text.name: help_moisture_grading_1
text.text: Harvested crops receive a quality score (0-100) based on their moisture content at harvest. Quality determines your sell price through per-crop grade bands (A through D). Each crop has different quality thresholds — some are more resilient to low quality than others.

text.name: help_moisture_grading_2
text.text: Quality degrades every hour when crops are stored above their ideal moisture level. The further above ideal, the faster quality drops. Once quality is lost, it cannot be recovered — but you can halt degradation by drying crops to their ideal moisture.

text.name: help_moisture_grading_3
text.text: View moisture-to-quality and quality-to-price tables in the Moisture Menu (Shift+M). Each crop has a unique ideal moisture range and unique price bands based on quality level.

text.name: help_moisture_drying_1
text.text: Crops stored above their ideal moisture level lose quality every hour. To prevent this, use silo drying: walk up to any silo on foot and activate drying. The dryer runs hourly, reducing moisture until all crops reach their ideal level, then stops automatically.

text.name: help_moisture_drying_2
text.text: Drying costs money based on volume and moisture removed, but is significantly cheaper than the drying charges incurred when selling wet crops directly. Larger volumes take longer to dry (the rate slows with more material).

text.name: help_moisture_drying_3
text.text: When selling crops above ideal moisture at a selling station, you will be charged a separate 'Drying Charges' fee in addition to the reduced quality-based price. Dry your crops before selling to avoid these charges entirely.

text.name: help_moisture_grass_1
text.text: Manage hay production with realistic grass drying mechanics. Use a tedder to spread and aerate grass, reducing its moisture content. You can ted multiple times for faster drying! Weather affects drying - sunshine accelerates it while rain increases moisture.

text.name: help_moisture_grass_2
text.text: When grass dries sufficiently (below 7% moisture), it automatically converts to hay. If hay gets too wet again (above 7% moisture), it converts back to grass.

text.name: help_moisture_grass_3
text.text: Grass piles accumulate rain exposure time when left on the ground during wet weather. After enough exposure, grass begins to rot and gradually lose volume. Collect or bale your grass promptly to avoid losses.

text.name: help_moisture_groundrot_1
text.text: Both grass and straw piles left on the ground track rain exposure. Piles can tolerate some rain before degradation begins (grace period), but the longer material has been exposed to rain, the faster it rots.

text.name: help_moisture_groundrot_2
text.text: Piles slowly dry when rain stops, but once rotting starts, it continues. Rotting material gradually disappears and loses volume. Collect or bale your straw and grass promptly to avoid losses, especially before bad weather.

text.name: help_moisture_bales_1
text.text: Bales left exposed to weather gradually accumulate rain exposure time when uncovered during wet weather. Bales can tolerate some exposure before rotting begins - this initial phase is called 'Getting Wet'.

text.name: help_moisture_bales_2
text.text: After the grace period, bales enter progressive rotting states: Rotting Slowly, Rotting, and Rotting Quickly. The longer bales have been exposed in the past, the faster they rot. Bales slowly dry out when rain stops, but once rotting begins, they cannot recover and will gradually lose volume.

text.name: help_moisture_bales_3
text.text: Protection: Wrapped bales and bales stored under shelter are protected from weather. Store bales indoors, wrap them, or sell them promptly to prevent losses.

text.name: help_moisture_meter_1
text.text: The moisture meter is a hand tool available in the Handtools section of the shop. To use it: Buy the moisture meter, equip it from your hand tool menu, then press and hold the action button for 4 seconds to get a moisture reading at your current location.

text.name: help_moisture_meter_2
text.text: Alternatively, you can enable 'Show Field Moisture' in the game settings to see moisture in the field info HUD without using the hand tool. This is useful for quick checks while operating equipment.

text.name: help_moisture_settings_1
text.text: Choose from three climate presets in the game settings: Dry (lower moisture levels), Normal (balanced, default), or Wet (higher moisture levels). Each preset affects monthly moisture ranges throughout the year.

text.name: help_moisture_settings_2
text.text: Fine-tune behavior with adjustable settings: Moisture Loss/Gain Multipliers control drying and wetting speed, Tedding Moisture Reduction adjusts drying per pass, Quality Decay Rate controls how fast quality degrades, and Drying Speed controls silo drying rate.

text.name: help_moisture_settings_3
text.text: Other settings include Sell Drying Charge Rate (cost when selling wet crops), Bale Rotting settings, Field Moisture Display toggle, and Moisture Meter Reporting style. In multiplayer, the host/admin controls these settings.
